##NAMES=Relianceite-(K)
##RRUFFID=R250049
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=K_4_Mg(V^4+^O)_2_(C_2_O_4_)(PO_3_OH)_4_(H_2_O)_10_
##LOCALITY=Rowley mine, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Sky blue prisms  that occur in bat-guano-related, post-mining assemblage of phases, associated with antipinite, dendoraite-(NH4) (R250048), fluorite, mimetite, mottramite, rowleyite, salammoniac, struvite, vanadinite, willemite, wulfenite and at least one other new mineral. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2022)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250049
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[K_2.21_(NH_4_)_1.79_]Mg_0.96_(V^4+^_0.95_O)_2_(C_2_O_4_)[P_1.03_O_3.03_(OH)_0.97_]_4_(H_2_O)_10_ ; The sample suffered loss of much of the weakly held H_2_O and probably a portion of its NH_4_. Relianceite-(K) was very sensitive to the electron beam and additional loss of these components probably occurred during the EPMA. The very large loss in H_2_O resulted in much higher concentrations   for the remaining constituents than are to be expected for the fully hydrated phase; therefore, the other analyzed constituents have been normalized to provide a total of 100% when combined with the calculated H_2_O content. To account for the loss of NH_4_, (NH_4_)_2_O was calculated so that K + NH_4_ = 4 atoms per formula unit (apfu) in accord with the structure.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, run in Gandolfi mode. Diffraction profile is calculated with Cu radiation
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.424 b: 9.03 c: 13.273 alpha: 90 beta: 100.75 gamma: 90 volume: 1463 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
